twitter_thread: 85% of my $1k home office budget was wasted on 5 gadgets that didn't work. Only 2 did: a $15 LED desk lamp and a $20 keyboard tray.
Ditched my old mouse for a $25 vertical one and cut wrist pain from 8/10 to 1.6/10 in 14 days.
Swapped my $100 office chair for a $30 balance ball and saw a 22% boost in daily tasks completed.
93% of my home office issues stemmed from 3 mistakes: dim lighting, poor ergonomics, and clutter.
Saved 72 minutes/week with a $10 cable organizer and a 'touch once' filing system.
Spent $50 on a noise-cancelling headset and now get 30% fewer 'can you repeat that?'s on calls.
What's the one home office gadget you rely on daily? #homeoffice #productivity
pack.linkedin: I've spent $1k on 7 home office gadgets, with 5 being a complete waste. However, a $15 LED desk lamp and a $20 keyboard tray have been crucial in increasing my productivity. I've also switched to a vertical mouse, reducing my wrist pain by 80% in 2 weeks. Insufficient lighting, poor ergonomics, and clutter have been my top 3 hurdles. What are the essential items you've found to be vital to your home office setup?
tiktok_hooks: I blew a thousand bucks on home office junk, but found two gems
I trashed my fancy chair for a $30 balance ball and my back thanks me
My $1k home office experiment proved it's the cheap stuff that works
